children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology (in this chapter referred to as the “Director”) is authorized to establish National Construction Safety Teams (in this chapter referred to as a “Team”) for deployment after events causing the failure of a building or buildings that has resulted in substantial loss of life or that posed significant potential for substantial loss of life. To the maximum extent practicable, the Director shall establish and deploy a Team within 48 hours after such an event. The Director shall promptly publish in the Federal Register notice of the establishment of each Team.

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 1 Purpose
children.text: The purpose of investigations by Teams is to improve the safety and structural integrity of buildings in the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: establish the likely technical cause or causes of the building failure;

children.label: B
children.text: evaluate the technical aspects of evacuation and emergency response procedures;

children.label: C
children.text: recommend, as necessary, specific improvements to building standards, codes, and practices based on the findings made pursuant to subparagraphs (A) and (B); and

children.label: D
children.text: recommend any research and other appropriate actions needed to improve the structural safety of buildings, and improve evacuation and emergency response procedures, based on the findings of the investigation.

children.label: A
children.text: regarding conflicts of interest related to service on the Team;

children.label: B
children.text: defining the circumstances under which the Director will establish and deploy a Team;

children.label: C
children.text: prescribing the appropriate size of Teams;

children.label: D
children.text: guiding the disclosure of information under ;section 7306 of this title

children.label: E
children.text: guiding the conduct of investigations under this chapter, including procedures for providing written notice of inspection authority under and for ensuring compliance with any other applicable law;section 7303(a) of this title

children.label: F
children.text: identifying and prescribing appropriate conditions for the provision by the Director of additional resources and services Teams may need;

children.label: G
children.text: to ensure that investigations under this chapter do not impede and are coordinated with any search and rescue efforts being undertaken at the site of the building failure;

children.label: H
children.text: for regular briefings of the public on the status of the investigative proceedings and findings;

children.label: I
children.text: guiding the Teams in moving and preserving evidence as described in section 7303(a)(4), (b)(2), and (d)(4) of this title;

children.label: J
children.text: providing for coordination with Federal, State, and local entities that may sponsor research or investigations of building failures, including research conducted under the Earthquake Hazards Reduction Act of 1977 [ et seq.]; and42 U.S.C. 7701

children.label: K
children.text: regarding such other issues as the Director considers appropriate.

children.heading: Publication
children.label: 2 Publication
children.text: The Director shall publish promptly in the Federal Register final procedures, and subsequent updates thereof, developed under paragraph (1).
